tags.name: Index
tags.description: Operations regarding adding, updating or deleting documents

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Operations about performing searches over tables

externalDocs.description: Find out more about Manticore Search

field.description: Name of field to operate with

aggCompositeTerm.description: Object representing a term to be used in composite aggregation.

aggCompositeSource.description: Object containing terms used for composite aggregation.

aggHistogram.description: Object to use histograms in aggregation, i.e., grouping search results by histogram values

offset.description: Offset of the histogram values. Default value is 0.

keyed.description: Flag that defines if a search response will be a dictionary with the bucket keys. Default value is false.

aggDateHistogram.description: Object to use histograms in aggregation, i.e., grouping search results by histogram values

offset.description: Offset of the histogram values. Default value is 0.

keyed.description: Flag that defines if a search response will be a dictionary with the bucket keys. Default value is false.

aggComposite.description: Object to perform composite aggregation, i.e., grouping search results by multiple fields

size.description: Maximum number of composite buckets in the result

items.description: List of objects that contain terms used for composite aggregation.

field.description: Name of attribute to aggregate by

size.description: Maximum number of buckets in the result

aggTerms.description: Object containing term fields to aggregate on

allOf.description: Object used for grouping search results

field.description: Field to perform the k-nearest neighbor search on

k.description: Deprecated. Use the top-level `limit` parameter instead.

query_vector.description: The vector used as input for the KNN search

doc_id.description: The docuemnt ID used as input for the KNN search

ef.description: Optional parameter controlling the accuracy of the search

knn.description: Object representing a k-nearest neighbor search query

location_anchor.description: Specifies the location of the pin point used for search

location_source.description: Field name in the document that contains location data

distance_type.description: Algorithm used to calculate the distance

distance.description: The distance from the anchor point to filter results by

geoDistance.description: Object to perform geo-distance based filtering on queries

searchQuery.description: Defines a query structure for performing search operations

table.description: The table to perform the search on

join.description: Join clause to combine search data from multiple tables

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

aggs.description: Defines aggregation settings for grouping results

expressions.description: Expressions to calculate additional values for the result

max_matches.description: Maximum number of matches allowed in the result

offset.description: Starting point for pagination of the result

profile.description: Enable or disable profiling of the search request

sort.description: Sorting criteria for the search results

_source.description: Specify which fields to include or exclude in the response

track_scores.description: Enable or disable result weight calculation used for sorting

autocompleteRequest.description: Object containing the data for performing an autocomplete search.

table.description: The table to perform the search on

query.description: The beginning of the string to autocomplete

options.description:
Autocomplete options
  - layouts: A comma-separated string of keyboard layout codes to validate and check for spell correction. Available options - us, ru, ua, se, pt, no, it, gr, uk, fr, es, dk, de, ch, br, bg, be. By default, all are enabled.
  - fuzziness: (0,1 or 2) Maximum Levenshtein distance for finding typos. Set to 0 to disable fuzzy matching. Default is 2
  - prepend: true/false If true, adds an asterisk before the last word for prefix expansion (e.g., *word )
  - append:  true/false If true, adds an asterisk after the last word for prefix expansion (e.g., word* )
  - expansion_len: Number of characters to expand in the last word. Default is 10.

must.description: Query clauses that must match for the document to be included

must_not.description: Query clauses that must not match for the document to be included

should.description: Query clauses that should be matched, but are not required

bulkResponse.description: Success response for bulk search requests

errors.description: Errors occurred during the bulk operation

error.description: Error message describing an error if such occurred

current_line.description: Number of the row returned in the response

skipped_lines.description: Number of rows skipped in the response

deleteDocumentRequest.description:
Payload for delete request.
Documents can be deleted either one by one by specifying the document id or by providing a query object.
For more information see  [Delete API](https://manual.manticoresearch.com/Deleting_documents)

id.description: The ID of document for deletion

query.description: Defines the criteria to match documents for deletion

deleteResponse.description: Response object for successful delete request

table.description: The name of the table from which the document was deleted

id.description: The ID of the deleted document. If multiple documents are deleted, the ID of the first deleted document is returned

found.description: Indicates whether any documents to be deleted were found

result.description: Result of the delete operation, typically 'deleted'

allOf.description: Defines a query HIGHLIGHT expression to emphasize matched results

after_match.description: Text inserted after the matched term, typically used for HTML formatting

allow_empty.description: Permits an empty string to be returned as the highlighting result. Otherwise, the beginning of the original text would be returned

around.description: Number of words around the match to include in the highlight

before_match.description: Text inserted before the match, typically used for HTML formatting

emit_zones.description: Emits an HTML tag with the enclosing zone name before each highlighted snippet

encoder.description: If set to 'html', retains HTML markup when highlighting

force_all_words.description: Ignores the length limit until the result includes all keywords

force_snippets.description: Forces snippet generation even if limits allow highlighting the entire text

highlight_query.description: Optional query object to customize highlighting behavior

html_strip_mode.description: Defines the mode for handling HTML markup in the highlight

limits_per_field.description: Determines whether the 'limit', 'limit_words', and 'limit_snippets' options operate as individual limits in each field of the document

no_match_size.description: If set to 1, allows an empty string to be returned as a highlighting result

order.description: Sets the sorting order of highlighted snippets

pre_tags.description: Text inserted before each highlighted snippet

post_tags.description: Text inserted after each highlighted snippet

start_snippet_id.description: Sets the starting value of the %SNIPPET_ID% macro

use_boundaries.description: Defines whether to additionally break snippets by phrase boundary characters

highlightFields.description: List of fields available for highlighting

fragment_size.description: Maximum size of the text fragments in highlighted snippets per field

limit.description: Maximum size of snippets per field

limit_snippets.description: Maximum number of snippets per field

limit_words.description: Maximum number of words per field

number_of_fragments.description: Total number of highlighted fragments per field

highlightFieldOption.description: Options for controlling the behavior of highlighting on a per-field basis

_id.description: The ID of the matched document

_score.description: The score of the matched document

_source.description: The source data of the matched document

_knn_dist.description: The knn distance of the matched document returned for knn queries

highlight.description: The highlighting-related data of the matched document

table.description: The table name of the matched document returned for percolate queries

_type:.description: The type of the matched document returned for percolate queries

fields.description: The percolate-related fields of the matched document returned for percolate queries

hitsHits.description: Search hit representing a matched document

insertDocumentRequest.description: Object containing data for inserting a new document into the table

table.description: Name of the table to insert the document into

cluster.description: Name of the cluster to insert the document into

id.description: Document ID. If not provided, an ID will be auto-generated

true.description: List of objects defining joined tables

table.description: Basic table of the join operation

allOf.description: Object representing the join operation between two tables

joinCond.description: Object representing the conditions used to perform the join operation

_match.description: Filter helper object defining a match keyword and match options

match_all.description: Filter helper object defining the 'match all'' condition

range.description: Filter helper object defining the 'range' condition

query_string.description: Filter object defining a query string

match.description: Filter object defining a match keyword passed as a string or in a Match object

match_phrase.description: Filter object defining a match phrase

match_all.description: Filter object to select all documents

fulltextFilter.description: Defines a type of filter for full-text search queries

equals.description: Filter to match exact attribute values.

in.description: Filter to match a given set of attribute values.

range.description: Filter to match a given range of attribute values passed in Range objects

queryFilter.description: Object used to apply various conditions, such as full-text matching or attribute filtering, to a search query

replaceDocumentRequest.description: Object containing the document data for replacing an existing document in a table.

doc.description: Object containing the new document data to replace the existing one.

sourceRules.description: Defines which fields to include or exclude in the response for a search query

includes.description: List of fields to include in the response

excludes.description: List of fields to exclude from the response

successResponse.description: Response object indicating the success of an operation, such as inserting or updating a document

id.description: ID of the document affected by the request operation

created.description: Indicates whether the document was created as a result of the operation

result.description: Result of the operation, typically 'created', 'updated', or 'deleted'

found.description: Indicates whether the document was found in the table

status.description: HTTP status code representing the result of the operation

percolateRequest.description: Object containing the query for percolating documents against stored queries in a percolate table

percolate.description: Object representing the document to percolate

searchResponse.description: Response object containing the results of a search request

took.description: Time taken to execute the search

timed_out.description: Indicates whether the search operation timed out

aggregations.description: Aggregated search results grouped by the specified criteria

max_score.description: Maximum score among the matched documents

total_relation.description: Indicates whether the total number of hits is accurate or an estimate

hits.description: Array of hit objects, each representing a matched document
Object containing the search hits, which represent the documents that matched the query.

profile.description: Profile information about the search execution, if profiling is enabled

scroll.description: Scroll token to be used fo pagination

warning.description: Warnings encountered during the search operation

responseErrorDetails.description: Detailed error information returned in case of an error response

type.description: Type or category of the error

reason.description: Detailed explanation of why the error occurred

table.description: The table related to the error, if applicable

responseErrorText.description: Error message text returned in case of an error

errorResponse.description: Error response object containing information about the error and a status code

status.description: HTTP status code of the error response

sqlResponse.description: List of responses from executed SQL queries

updateDocumentRequest.description: Payload for updating a document or multiple documents in a table

doc.description: Object containing the document fields to update

query.description: Object defining conditions to perform the updates

updateResponse.description: Success response returned after updating one or more documents

result.description: Result of the update operation, typically 'updated'

post.summary: Performs a search on a table
post.description:
The method expects an object with the following mandatory properties:
* the name of the table to search
* the match query object
For details, see the documentation on [**SearchRequest**](SearchRequest.md)
The method returns an object with the following properties:
- took: the time taken to execute the search query. - timed_out: a boolean indicating whether the query timed out. - hits: an object with the following properties:
   - total: the total number of hits found.
   - hits: an array of hit objects, where each hit object represents a matched document. Each hit object has the following properties:
     - _id: the ID of the matched document.
     - _score: the score of the matched document.
     - _source: the source data of the matched document.

In addition, if profiling is enabled, the response will include an additional array with profiling information attached. Also, if pagination is enabled, the response will include an additional 'scroll' property with a scroll token to use for pagination
Here is an example search response:

  ```
  {
    'took':10,
    'timed_out':false,
    'hits':
    {
      'total':2,
      'hits':
      [
        {'_id':'1','_score':1,'_source':{'gid':11}},
        {'_id':'2','_score':1,'_source':{'gid':12}}
      ]
    }
  }
  ```

For more information about the match query syntax and additional parameters that can be added to request and response, please see the documentation [here](https://manual.manticoresearch.com/Searching/Full_text_matching/Basic_usage#HTTP-JSON).

post.summary: Performs an autocomplete search on a table
Delete a document in a table
Create a new document in a table
post.description:
Insert a document. 
Expects an object like:
 
  ```
  {
    'table':'movies',
    'id':701,
    'doc':
    {
      'title':'This is an old movie',
      'plot':'A secret team goes to North Pole',
      'year':1950,
      'rating':9.5,
      'lat':60.4,
      'lon':51.99,
      'advise':'PG-13',
      'meta':'{"keywords":{"travel","ice"},"genre":{"adventure"}}',
      'language':[2,3]
    }
  }
  ```
 
The document id can also be missing, in which case an autogenerated one will be used:
         
  ```
  {
    'table':'movies',
    'doc':
    {
      'title':'This is a new movie',
      'plot':'A secret team goes to North Pole',
      'year':2020,
      'rating':9.5,
      'lat':60.4,
      'lon':51.99,
      'advise':'PG-13',
      'meta':'{"keywords":{"travel","ice"},"genre":{"adventure"}}',
      'language':[2,3]
    }
  }
  ```
 
It responds with an object in format:
  
  ```
  {'table':'products','id':701,'created':true,'result':'created','status':201}
  ```

post.summary: Perform reverse search on a percolate table
Replace new document in a table
post.description:
Replace an existing document. Input has same format as `insert` operation.
Responds with an object in format:

  ```
  {'table':'products','id':1,'created':false,'result':'updated','status':200}
  ```

Run a query in SQL format.
Expects a query string passed through `body` parameter and optional `raw_response` parameter that defines a format of response.
`raw_response` can be set to `False` for Select queries only, e.g., `SELECT * FROM mytable`
The query string must stay as it is, no URL encoding is needed.
The response object depends on the query executed. In select mode the response has same format as `/search` operation.

parameters.name: raw_response
parameters.description: Optional parameter, defines a format of response. Can be set to `False` for Select only queries and set to `True` for any type of queries. Default value is 'True'.

200.description:
In case of SELECT-only in mode none the response schema is the same as of `search`.
In case of `mode=raw` or `raw_response=true` the response depends on the query executed.

post.summary: Update a document in a table
Partially replaces a document in a table
post.description:
Partially replaces a document with given id in a table
Responds with an object of the following format: 

  ```
  {'table':'products','updated':1}
  ```

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the document to replace
